pages.text: Where the Digester turns matter into fuel, the $(flesh)Decomposer$() turns matter into $(italic)parts$(). It breaks items down into the raw biomancy components — flesh bits, bone fragments, fibres, bile — that the Bio-Forge spends.
pages.title: The Decomposer

pages.text: A Living Flesh core, a $(italic)Bile Gland$(), raw meat and two fangs at a bench — or, far cheaper, grown in the Bio-Forge from flesh bits, bone fragments, fibres and mineral fragments.

$(bold)Why it matters.$() The $(l:1_harvest/flesh_plunderer)harvest$() gets you started, but hunting does not scale. The Decomposer does: feed it bulk material and it returns a steady stream of the fibres and fragments the Forge needs, with no mob in sight.$(br2)Its outputs are ranged — a given input may yield zero to several of a part — so think of it in averages, and run it on a stockpile.
pages.title: The Workhorse
